critic_raw:
BASELINE ASSESSMENT:

Three problems prevent attributing the 23% chargeback reduction to the new fraud model.

First, the holiday routing policy auto-declined high-loss merchant categories starting at the same time as the model launch. Chargebacks require transactions to clear — auto-declining high-loss merchants removes those transactions from the fraud pipeline entirely. This is a direct chargeback reduction mechanism that has nothing to do with the model's ranking ability.

Second, the pre/post comparison spans a seasonal transition from the quiet early-fall period to the high-volume holiday fraud season. These have different merchant mixes, different fraud rates by category, different transaction volume, and different fraudster tactics. The comparison is not controlled for seasonal effects.

Third, both models were not evaluated on the same transaction stream under the same downstream rules. The observed difference could reflect any combination of model quality, policy change, and seasonal fraud mix — these effects are entangled in the production metric.

The model's architectural improvements and fresher training data are real but do not establish the causal claim. A same-traffic, fixed-policy backtest is required.

Verdict: critique_wins.
